title: navarretia pubescens var. tagetina
intro: Navarretia pubescens var. tagetina, also known as the sand showy false pennyroyal or sand-skipper plant, is a variety of flowering plant in the phlox family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antispasmodic, carminative, and expectorant, helping to relieve muscle spasms, gas, and congestion. The health benefits of Navarretia pubescens var. tagetina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in a tincture or salve.
